---
title: "Rattlesnake master (Eryngium yuccifolium)"
slug: "eryngium-yuccifolium"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://static.inaturalist.org/photos/622614113/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Rattlesnake master"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Apiales"
tax_family: "Apiaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Apiales", "Apiaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://static.inaturalist.org/photos/622614113/medium.jpg" alt="Rattlesnake master" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/stevecollins">
stevecollins</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341953322">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Apiales
-   **Family:** Apiaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=128695)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not available
-   **Rarity note:** Rare across Florida.

## Natural history notes

A distinctive wildflower characterized by its stiff, yucca-like leaves and round, spiny white flower heads. In Florida, it is found in open habitats such as flatwoods, prairies, and savannas. It typically blooms in summer and attracts a wide variety of pollinators, including bees, butterflies, and wasps.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/128695-Eryngium-yuccifolium)
-   [Local profile](https://www.flawildflowers.org/flower-friday-eryngium-yuccifolium/)
